task_completion.description: The agent placed at least 5 new interior partition walls and at least 4 doors to subdivide the space.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan or 3D view in Sweet Home 3D. There should be multiple new interior walls dividing the open shell, and at least 4 doors (or windows) placed within or along these walls.

task_completion.description: The agent defined and named at least 5 distinct rooms (e.g., Dorm Room, Lounge, Bathroom).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan. There should be at least 5 distinct areas with room name text labels (e.g., 'Dorm Room', 'Bathroom', 'Lounge', 'Kitchen') visible on the grid.

task_completion.description: The sleeping quarters are furnished with 16 beds, 8 desks, 8 chairs, and 4 shelves/wardrobes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan, 3D view, or the furniture list panel on the bottom left. Count approximately 16 beds, 8 desks, 8 chairs, and 4 storage units (shelves/wardrobes) placed in the designated dorm room areas.

task_completion.description: The bathrooms are equipped with at least 4 toilets and 4 sinks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bathroom areas in the 2D or 3D view, or check the furniture list panel. There should be at least 4 toilets and 4 sinks visible.

task_completion.description: The common lounge has 2 sofas, 2 tables, and 2 lamps, and the kitchen has 3 appliances and 1 dining table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the lounge and kitchen areas or the furniture list panel. Verify the presence of at least 2 sofas, 2 tables, 2 lamps, 3 kitchen appliances (e.g., fridge, oven, stove), and 1 dining table.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to build the dormitory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the floor plan being actively edited, rather than just terminal commands or text editors modifying the .sh3d XML directly.

integrity.description: The agent placed furniture items using the catalog rather than just renaming existing items.
integrity.visual_evidence: The furniture catalog on the top left should be visible, and items in the plan should match standard Sweet Home 3D 3D models, not just text placeholders.

integrity.description: The agent saved the completed project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D window title should not have an asterisk (*) at the end of the filename in the final screenshots, or there should be evidence of the 'Save' dialog being used.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The pass threshold for this task is 70/100 points. The task requires strictly quantified furniture: 16 beds, 8 desks, 8 chairs, 4 shelves/wardrobes, 4 toilets, 4 sinks, 2 sofas, 2 tables, 2 lamps, 3 kitchen appliances, and 1 dining table. For the '4 doors' requirement, placing windows also counts as valid (as they share the doorOrWindow='true' attribute in the backend). There is a wrong-target gate: if fewer than 10 total furniture items are placed across the entire file, the agent automatically fails.
